page: Microsoft Azure D48d_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the DDv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio of 4.0 GB per core. Additionally, it includes 1843 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, offering high-capacity local disk space for temporary databases and caching.

Benchmark results place the D48d_v5 in the average to strong performance tiers. In CPU-bound tasks, the instance achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1638.0 and a multi-core score of 15463.0. It demonstrates strong capabilities in specific workloads, such as image processing with a Geekbench score of 14999.0 and cryptographic operations with a PassMark CPU encryption score of 42548.75, both ranking in the top 25% tier. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only large block bandwidth of 152.26 GB/sec and a write bandwidth of 65.92 GB/sec.

Given its balanced resource allocation and substantial local storage, the D48d_v5 is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring high-capacity local scratch space alongside steady compute power. It is well-suited for relational databases, enterprise applications, web serving, and software compilation, as evidenced by its Clang compilation score of 36367.0. The lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, though it maintains respectable CPU-based LLM inference speeds, achieving 285.96 tokens/sec on text generation with a small 135M model.
description: Microsoft Azure D48d_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 48 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and includes 1843 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Performance benchmarks indicate solid capabilities, with strong results in image processing and cryptographic encryption tests, both placing in the top 25% tier. The instance delivers a read-only large block memory bandwidth of 152.26 GB/sec and a multi-core Geekbench score of 15463.0. Lacking a dedicated GPU, the D48d_v5 is optimized for compute-balanced and storage-reliant workloads. It is highly suitable for hosting relational databases, enterprise application servers, software compilation pipelines, and high-throughput web serving environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48d_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and 1843 GB of local storage, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor for balanced enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48d_v5 is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 1843 GB local storage, powered by an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring forty-eight vCPUs, high-capacity local SSD storage, and strong cryptographic and image processing performance.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 192 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor
1843 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage included
Strong performance in cryptographic encryption and image processing benchmarks
Balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio suitable for general-purpose workloads
Ideal for relational databases, enterprise applications, and software compilation
